title: Seeds and Fruits Limited to two types or
display_title: Seeds and Fruits Limited to two types or
wikitext:
[]: Seeds & Fruits (Limited to two types, or...?)             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 10, 2008 11:31 am BANANA SPLIT V wrote: The First Ancestral Race made two kinds of Seed of Life: "Fruit of Knowledge-type" (such as Lilith) and "Fruit of Life-type" (such as Adam)

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Mar 10, 2008 11:52 am Ornette wrote: Remember that "Fruit of Life" and "Fruit of Knowledge" are names given by a group of lazy writers trying to look cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:14 pm But there is an in-universe justification for it, so you can only take your bitterness and cynicism SO far.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:36 pm Again, you assume that there are only 2 kinds of seeds. Remember that "Fruit of Life" and "Fruit of Knowledge" are names given by a group of religious fanatics.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:45 pm ("Living god"? Is this some dub thing?) In a theoretical situation, you could bring any two Seeds together and end up with something more uber than either one is alone. This fusion would be "forbidden" because no two Seeds are meant to share the same planet and all that jive. As for the respective "fruits" (whatever exactly a "fruit" is) they defer onto their respective offspring, we could also theorize that any two combined in a single lifeform would produce an entity that is transcendent to an entity possessing only one. Additionally, the term "god" is, ultimately, not very meaningful, especially in this show and its expanded canon, wherein "god" is batted around like a Ping-Pong ball. All you really need is for somebody to quantify what a "god" is, and then creating one is just a matter of following the recipe.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:48 pm V wrote: Again, you assume that there are only 2 kinds of seeds. Remember that "Fruit of Life" and "Fruit of Knowledge" are names given by a group of religious fanatics.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 1:52 pm Actually the way I see it it wouldn't really be much of a problem if two "Adams" landed on the same planet.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 2:11 pm It still isn't within parameters -- 1 planet:1 Seed. Additionally, the concept of truly identical Seeds is an assumption only. Each one may be unique to the point of being "incompatible" with any other.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Mar 10, 2008 6:38 pm They might all be incompatible, but we come down to the basic dichotomy: "are they intelligent science-using beings benefitting from Knowledge?" or "Are they awesomely powerful but non-social beings benefiting from the Power of Life"? Of course, two Liliths landing on one planet would have made for a more boring story, even if they weren't "compatible"...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 10, 2008 6:38 pm We know that there is at least two seeds. Any other seeds is completely unknown to us and is as alien as Martians. Seed of Life and Seed of Knowledge. What other kind of seeds could there be? Seed of Growing Fruit?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:06 pm Your making even more assumptions here. First, to clarify (or repeat, again) what I've mentioned about the "fruit of life"/"fruit of knowledge". The actual concepts which these 2 things embody were given names, most likely by religious zealots (e.g. Seele). Within the canon, they are NEVER referred to as what they actually embody, but by these names. The assumption that I'm talking about is that there doesn't even have to be 1 type "fruit of life", or 1 type of "fruit of knowledge". And I'm referring to what they actually embody, not the name that Seele gave them. A counter argument would be, do you think 2 seeds of life whose spawn have the ability to benefit from knowlege are compatible? No. That assumption can't be made. There's nothing except for the 2 examples seen on Earth. To assume that there are only these 2 types, and of each of these 2 types, all of the seeds that share the same type is identical, on the basis of the names that were given to them by Seele, is absurd. And by names, this is inclusive of the babble during EoE, which seems more metaphorical than descriptional of what's actually happening.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:31 pm I'm not assuming anything. I'm simply saying that any other seeds are completely unknown to us and are irrelevant.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:37 pm If one poses the theoretical question, "Are there more types than those exemplified by Adam and Lilith?", the matter is relevant. (See thread title.) Are they relevant within the context of what's happening in the show itself? Not really, but if we restricted our discourse to what was strictly "relevant" that would be kind of boring, wouldn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:41 pm There is no evidence in the show saying that there are any others. And the only way there can be is if some major fanwankery took place (unless Rebuild has some more things to say about it).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 10, 2008 7:48 pm Technically, there's no evidence in the show that there are any other Seeds at all... In fact, the term and concept "Seed of Life" doesn't even come from the show itself. Uh-oh, don't tell me we're gonna go there .

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Mar 10, 2008 8:02 pm Lol, you jinxed it. Anyways, I've said this before, making only 2 types of Seeds makes no sense. It's taking an absurd chance because if one type turns out to be a total failure then there goes half your work. Diversity is your friend.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 10, 2008 8:05 pm That's why we don't make mistakes.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Mar 10, 2008 8:11 pm No decent scientist would take such a stupid chance in the first place. To put it in real world terms it'd be like making a flu vaccine for one type of flu under the assumption it would protect against all types. Such things just aren't done for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Tue Mar 11, 2008 12:04 am I wonder if all Seeds are equipped w/ either of the Fruits of Knowledge or the Fruits of Life...but every single Seed was different but all (genetically) similar. Would that fit?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Mar 11, 2008 12:53 am Again, making too many the same way is just asking for something to go wrong. Don't put all your eggs in one basket or so they say. The more diversity the better.
